blockingIssues.description: The milestone's coding-agent test suite is not fully green: `test/resource-loader.test.ts` fails 4 assertions, and two legacy permission suite files fail 4 assertions (8 total). These matched the mission's documented pre-existing baseline failures, so no new regressions were found in the reviewed refactor features, but the validator gate itself remains red.

appliedUpdates.description: Documented in `.factory/library/environment.md` that `npm run build` regenerates `packages/ai/src/models.generated.ts`, which can dirty an unrelated tracked file during coding-agent-only validation.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that mission-specific documented baseline failures override the generic 'return on out-of-scope validator failures' rule when the observed failure set matches exactly.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both completed refactor feature handoffs reported `followedProcedure: false` because the coding-agent-extension-worker skill said to return on failures outside the worker's changes, while the mission AGENTS explicitly allowed proceeding when the failure set matched the known 8 pre-existing failures.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify how workers should handle scoped dirty state already present in their assigned feature paths at session start after auditing that the changes match the assigned feature and can be fully validated.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `refactor-todotools-folder` handoff recorded a deliberate deviation because the worker found scoped in-progress changes in the assigned feature paths, validated them successfully, and committed them instead of immediately returning to the orchestrator.

orchestratorOverride.rationale: The validator gate failure is caused exclusively by the 8 mission-documented baseline failures in `test/resource-loader.test.ts` plus two legacy permission suite files. Both feature reviews passed, the targeted refactor scope is clean, the prompt section is byte-equivalent (verified by both the worker and a committed golden snapshot test), and `npm run build` and `npm run check` are green. Per mission AGENTS.md 'Known Pre-Existing Issues', these failures are out-of-scope for the todotools mission's extension-first fork strategy and must not be touched by mission features. The orchestrator therefore accepts the scrutiny milestone as effectively passed and marks the scrutiny feature `completed` without re-running. New regressions, if any, must still block; only the documented set is overridden.
orchestratorOverride.scopeOfOverride: Only the test validator gate for the refactor milestone is overridden. typecheck, lint, build, and both feature reviews are independently green and not part of the override.
guardrails: If any future scrutiny round on any milestone reports failures outside the documented baseline set, the override does not apply and must be re-evaluated.
If the documented baseline failure set ever changes (count or files), `mission AGENTS.md` must be updated and the override must be reconsidered before applying again.
